Name: International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO)
Eligibility: National team selection required; pre-university students
Description: Annual international mathematics competition for pre-university students, held since 1959; national teams compete in two exam papers.
Cost: Free (national selection process varies by country)
Notes: Team selection is handled by each country's national olympiad committee, not centrally; host country/dates change yearly.
Subject: Mathematics

Name: International Physics Olympiad (IPhO)
Eligibility: National team selection required; pre-university students
Description: Annual international physics competition for secondary school students, held since 1967.
Cost: Free (national selection process varies by country)
Notes: Selection handled nationally; host country/dates change yearly.
Subject: Physics

Name: International Chemistry Olympiad (IChO)
Organizer: International Chemistry Olympiad International Information Centre
Eligibility: National team selection required; secondary school students
Description: Annual international chemistry competition held since 1968, combining theoretical and experimental exams.
Cost: Free (national selection process varies by country)
Notes: Selection handled nationally; host country/dates change yearly.
Subject: Chemistry

Name: International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI)
Eligibility: National team selection required; pre-university students
Description: Annual international programming/algorithms competition for secondary school students, held since 1989.
Cost: Free (national selection process varies by country)
Notes: Selection handled nationally; host country/dates change yearly.
Subject: Computer Science

Name: International Biology Olympiad (IBO)
Eligibility: National team selection required; pre-university students under 20
Description: Annual international biology competition for secondary school students, held since 1990.
Cost: Free (national selection process varies by country)
Notes: Selection handled nationally; host country/dates change yearly.
Subject: Biology

Name: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF)
Eligibility: Must first win at a Society-affiliated local/regional/state/national science fair
Description: World's largest international pre-college science competition; finalists qualify through affiliated fairs in 60+ countries, regions, and territories.
Cost: Free to compete (affiliate fair/travel costs vary)
Notes: Entry is via affiliated local fairs, not direct application; affiliate fair deadlines vary by region.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: John Locke Institute Essay Competition (Global Essay Prize)
Eligibility: Open globally; no age restriction stated
Description: Annual essay competition across philosophy, politics, economics, history, psychology, theology, and law; participants from 100+ countries.
Notes: The 2026 cycle is closed (registration by March 31, submission by May 31, 2026). Runs annually; the 2027 cycle typically opens registration in early February with a late-May submission deadline. Free to enter by the regular deadline.
Subject: Writing; Social Sciences & Humanities; Economics & Business

Name: World Scholar's Cup
Eligibility: Teams of three students; three age divisions
Description: Global academic competition combining a quiz bowl, collaborative writing, team debate, and a Tournament of Champions at Yale University; regional rounds in 70+ countries.
Notes: Regional round dates/fees vary by location; check local round listing.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Research Science Institute (RSI)
Organizer: Center for Excellence in Education (CEE), hosted at MIT
Country_Region: United States (international students apply via home-country selection)
Eligibility: Rising high school seniors aged 16+ by July 1. Open to U.S. citizens/permanent residents and to international students from participating countries (about 30 international places, via national selection).
Description: Free 6-week on-campus summer research program combining STEM coursework with mentored research; approximately 100 students selected annually.
Notes: Applications typically open in the fall with a mid-December deadline (RSI 2026 was December 10, 2025). RSI 2027 dates are announced by CEE in fall 2026. Highly selective; free to attend.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: PRIMES (Program for Research in Mathematics, Engineering and Science for High School Students)
Eligibility: High school sophomores/juniors; specific grades vary by track (MIT PRIMES, PRIMES-USA, PRIMES Circle, Yulia's Dream)
Description: Year-long mentored research program in mathematics, computer science, and computational biology for high school students.
Notes: The application link appears on the MIT PRIMES site in September; the deadline is typically around December 1 (PRIMES 2026 was December 1, 2025). Greater-Boston students for PRIMES; PRIMES-USA is open to U.S. students nationwide.
Subject: Mathematics; Computer Science

Name: Simons Summer Research Program
Eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents; high school juniors, at least 16 by program start, nominated by their school
Description: 7-week mentored research program pairing approximately 40 selected high school students with Stony Brook University faculty.
Notes: Requires school nomination (max 2 per school); highly selective (~5% acceptance rate).
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Summer Science Program (SSP)
Eligibility: High school juniors
Description: 5-week residential summer research program in astrophysics, biochemistry, bacterial genomics, or synthetic chemistry, held on partner college campuses.
Notes: Cost varies by track/campus; need-based financial aid available.
Subject: Earth & Space; Biology; Chemistry

Name: Yale Young Global Scholars (YYGS)
Eligibility: High school students from around the world (150+ countries)
Description: Two-week residential summer sessions at Yale across three tracks: Innovations in Science & Technology, Politics of Law & Economics, and Solving Global Challenges.
Cost: Paid (need-based financial aid and scholarships available)
Notes: Tuition varies by session; see site for current pricing and aid options.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: MITES (MIT Introduction to Technology, Engineering, and Science)
Country_Region: United States (MITES Saturdays limited to Boston/Cambridge/Lawrence, MA)
Eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents, 11th grade, for MITES Summer/Semester; MITES Saturdays limited to Boston-area public school students grades 7-10
Description: Free residential (Summer) or remote (Semester) STEM enrichment program at MIT for underrepresented high school students.
Notes: Multiple program tracks (Summer/Semester/Saturdays) with different eligibility and formats.
Subject: Engineering & Robotics; Interdisciplinary

Name: Davis United World College Scholars Program
Organizer: Administered by Middlebury College (founded by Shelby Davis and Phil Geier)
Country_Region: International (for UWC graduates attending US universities)
Eligibility: Graduates of the 18 United World College (UWC) high schools
Description: Need-based financial aid for UWC graduates pursuing higher education at 100+ partner colleges/universities in the United States.
Notes: Only open to UWC graduates, not the general public.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: QuestBridge National College Match
Eligibility: High school seniors currently attending high school in the U.S. (U.S. citizens/permanent residents living abroad are also eligible). Students living outside the U.S. are not eligible for the Match.
Description: Matches selected students with full four-year scholarships at QuestBridge partner colleges.
Notes: The 2026 National College Match application is due October 1, 2026 (11:59pm PT); match rankings due October 15. QuestBridge also runs the College Prep Scholarship for juniors with a late-March deadline.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Center for Talented Youth (CTY)
Eligibility: Grades 2-12; qualification via standardized test scores (e.g., 95th+ percentile for Advanced CTY Level)
Description: Academic enrichment courses (in-person and online) for advanced learners, spanning STEM and humanities subjects.
Cost: Paid, varies by course (e.g., ~$7,801 for a residential course); need-based financial aid available; $15 application fee
Notes: Tuition varies significantly by course/length; see course catalog for exact pricing.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Stanford Online High School
Eligibility: Academically advanced students, grades 7-12
Description: Accredited, fully online private high school with live seminar-style classes; awards approximately $2.5M annually in financial aid.
Notes: Full diploma-granting school, not a single course; admissions is selective.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Conrad Challenge
Eligibility: Ages 13-18, teams of 2-5 students
Description: Multi-stage innovation/entrepreneurship competition where student teams design solutions to real-world problems, culminating in an in-person Innovation Summit.
Cost: Free to start (Activation/Lean Canvas stages); $499/team for Innovation Stage; $499/person for in-person Summit; financial aid available
Notes: Runs on an annual season; the 2025-26 season opened August 28, 2025 (activation/registration through October 30) with the Innovation Summit in April 2026. The 2026-27 season is expected to open in late August 2026.
Subject: Engineering & Robotics; Economics & Business

Name: Technovation Girls
Eligibility: Girls/underrepresented genders in tech, team-based
Description: Global technology entrepreneurship program where student teams build mobile/AI solutions to community problems.
Notes: Program registration is managed via technovationchallenge.org; confirm current eligibility there.
Subject: Computer Science; Economics & Business

Name: The Concord Review
Eligibility: Secondary school students (history research papers)
Description: Quarterly journal publishing academic history research papers written by secondary school students; also operates the National Writing Board assessment service.
Notes: Fixed quarterly submission deadlines each year: February 1, May 1, August 1, and November 1. The next deadline is August 1, 2026 (Winter issue). Published quarterly.
Subject: Social Sciences & Humanities; Writing

Name: Journal of Student Research (High School Edition)
Eligibility: First author must be a high school student (grades 9-12) with a listed teacher/advisor
Description: Peer-reviewed, open-access multidisciplinary journal publishing high school, undergraduate, and graduate student research (AP Research, IB, honors research, etc.).
Cost: Paid Article Processing Charge: $50 at submission + $299 after acceptance
Notes: Double-blind peer review; APC charged in two phases.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Harvard Model United Nations (HMUN)
Eligibility: High school students; delegations from 60+ countries
Description: Large in-person Model UN conference held in Boston, run by Harvard undergraduates, simulating international diplomacy committees.
Notes: Held annually in late January; check official site for current session dates/fees.
Subject: Social Sciences & Humanities

Name: Regeneron Science Talent Search (STS)
Eligibility: U.S. high school seniors (and eligible U.S. students studying abroad) submitting original, independent research.
Description: The oldest and most prestigious pre-college science research competition in the U.S. (founded 1942). Entrants submit original research and a full application for holistic review; about $3.1 million in awards, with a top prize of $250,000.
Notes: The 2027 application is open June 1 - November 5, 2026 (8pm ET). Top 40 finalists compete in Washington, D.C., in March.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Breakthrough Junior Challenge
Eligibility: Students aged 13-18 worldwide; individual entry.
Description: Global science-communication competition in which students create an original short video (up to two minutes) explaining a concept in physics, the life sciences, or mathematics. The grand prize includes a $250,000 college scholarship, plus prizes for the student's teacher and school.
Notes: 2026 entries are due September 15, 2026 (11:59pm PDT); entrants must peer-review at least five other submissions by September 30.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Diamond Challenge
Eligibility: High school students aged 14-18, in teams of 2-4 with an adult advisor; open to any country.
Description: Global high school entrepreneurship competition in which teams develop an original business or social-venture concept and compete for a prize pool of about $100,000.
Notes: Runs annually; the 2026 cycle ran September 17, 2025 - January 15, 2026 with the summit in late April 2026. The next cycle typically opens in September with a mid-January deadline.
Subject: Economics & Business

Name: GENIUS Olympiad
Organizer: Terra Science and Education, with Rochester Institute of Technology
Eligibility: High school students (grades 9-12) worldwide, submitting projects related to environmental issues.
Description: International high school project competition focused on environmental issues, spanning science, engineering, art, short film, music, creative writing, robotics, and business categories. Finals are held at Rochester Institute of Technology in New York.
Notes: Runs annually; the 2026 project deadline was March 1, 2026 (extended to March 7) with finals in June 2026 at RIT. Check the official site for 2027 dates.
Subject: Environment; Interdisciplinary

Name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
Eligibility: U.S. high school seniors graduating in the 2026-2027 school year with a minimum 3.0 GPA; U.S. citizens or eligible status.
Description: Achievement-based scholarship that recognizes 150 U.S. high school seniors each year with a $20,000 college scholarship, selected for leadership, service, and academic excellence.
Notes: The 2027 application is open August 3 - September 30, 2026 (5pm ET). 150 scholars are selected annually.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: The Gates Scholarship
Eligibility: U.S. high school seniors from low-income households who are Pell-eligible and members of a minority ethnic group, with a strong academic record.
Description: Highly selective, last-dollar scholarship covering the full cost of attendance not met by other financial aid, for outstanding minority high school seniors from low-income households.
Notes: The 2027 application is available July 15 - September 15, 2026. A last-dollar award covering full cost of attendance beyond other aid.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: PROMYS (Program in Mathematics for Young Scientists)
Eligibility: Motivated high school students (typically age 15+) with a strong interest in mathematics.
Description: Six-week residential summer program at Boston University immersing students in deep exploration of number theory and creative mathematical problem-solving alongside mentors and researchers.
Notes: Held each summer at Boston University; the 2026 program ran June 28 - August 8 with a February 27, 2026 application deadline. The 2027 application typically opens in winter with a late-February/early-March deadline.
Subject: Mathematics

Name: Ross Mathematics Program
Eligibility: Pre-college students (typically age 15+) with a strong interest in mathematics.
Description: Intensive residential summer program (about six weeks) centered on deep exploration of number theory, emphasizing independent and abstract mathematical thinking.
Notes: Held each summer; the 2026 application deadline was around March 8, 2026 (now closed). The 2027 application typically opens in winter with an early-March deadline.
Subject: Mathematics

Name: COSMOS (California State Summer School for Mathematics and Science)
Eligibility: High school students (grades 8-12) with a strong record in math and science; primarily California residents, with out-of-state applicants accepted at some campuses.
Description: Four-week residential STEM summer program hosted across several University of California campuses, offering intensive clusters in advanced mathematics, science, and engineering.
Notes: Hosted at UC campuses including Davis, Irvine, San Diego, and Santa Cruz. The summer 2026 application closed February 6, 2026; the 2027 application typically opens in January with an early-February deadline.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Journal of Emerging Investigators (JEI)
Eligibility: Middle and high school students (grades 6-12), age 13+, of any nationality. Manuscripts must be submitted by an adult mentor or teacher.
Description: Open-access, peer-reviewed journal (founded by Harvard graduate students) that publishes original, hypothesis-driven research by middle and high school students in the life and physical sciences.
Notes: Rolling submissions; a single $49 fee covers the full review-and-publication process. As of March 2026, JEI focuses on hypothesis-driven life and experimental physical sciences (no purely computational or dataset-only projects).
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: National High School Journal of Science (NHSJS)
Organizer: National High School Journal of Science
Eligibility: High school students (grades 9-12); the primary author must be a high school student.
Description: Free, open-access, peer-reviewed online science journal run for and by high school students, publishing original research papers, reports, and short articles.
Notes: Rolling submissions with no submission or publication fees.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: Pioneer Academics
Eligibility: High school students worldwide (typically age 15+); selective admission.
Description: Accredited online research program that pairs high school students worldwide with university professors for independent research, culminating in a college-credit-bearing research paper.
Notes: Term-based rolling admission; attending an info session unlocks a priority deadline. On average, fewer than a third of applicants are admitted.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: CS50x: Introduction to Computer Science (HarvardX)
Eligibility: Open to all; no prerequisites (beginner-friendly).
Description: Harvard's free introduction to computer science and the art of programming (CS50x), covering C, Python, SQL, JavaScript, HTML/CSS, algorithms, and data structures. Self-paced, with a free audit track.
Notes: Self-paced with open enrollment via edX; the audit track (lectures and assignments) is free, with an optional paid verified certificate.
Subject: Computer Science

Name: International Olympiad on Astronomy and Astrophysics (IOAA)
Organizer: International Olympiad on Astronomy and Astrophysics
Eligibility: Secondary-school students selected through their national astronomy/astrophysics olympiad; about 50 countries participate.
Description: Annual international competition in astronomy and astrophysics for secondary-school students, with theoretical, data-analysis, and observational rounds.
Notes: Participation is through each country's national olympiad selection; the 19th IOAA is in Hanoi, Vietnam. Turkiye competes via its national astronomy olympiad.
Subject: Earth & Space; Physics

Name: International Linguistics Olympiad (IOL)
Eligibility: Secondary-school students selected through their national linguistics olympiad; 40+ countries and territories.
Description: One of the international science olympiads: students solve self-contained linguistic puzzles from a wide range of languages using logic and reasoning, in individual and team contests.
Notes: Entry is via national linguistics olympiad selection; the 23rd IOL is in Bucharest, Romania.
Subject: Linguistics

Name: International Geography Olympiad (iGeo)
Organizer: International Geographical Union (IGU) Olympiad Task Force
Eligibility: Students aged 16-19 selected through their national geography olympiad; about 50 countries.
Description: Annual competition for 16-19-year-old geography students, comprising a written test, a multimedia test, and substantial fieldwork.
Notes: Entry is via national geography olympiad selection. iGeo 2026 is hosted in Istanbul, Turkiye.
Subject: Earth & Space; Social Sciences & Humanities

Name: European Girls' Mathematical Olympiad (EGMO)
Eligibility: Female secondary-school students selected through their national mathematical olympiad; 60+ countries, including many beyond Europe.
Description: Premier international mathematical olympiad for female secondary-school students, with two exam papers held over two days.
Notes: Entry is via each country's national mathematical olympiad selection for female students; 67 countries participated in 2026. Turkiye participates.
Subject: Mathematics

Name: London International Youth Science Forum (LIYSF)
Eligibility: Students aged 16-21 with a strong interest in science; open worldwide (80+ countries).
Description: Two-week residential STEM summer forum in London with lectures by leading scientists, lab visits, and demonstrations, bringing together about 500 students from 80+ countries.
Notes: Apply by about 31 May each year (the 2026 deadline was 31 May; program 19 Jul - 1 Aug 2026). On-campus and virtual options available.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: FIRST Global Challenge
Eligibility: High school students (ages ~14-18); one national team per country, selected by the national organization; 190+ countries.
Description: Olympics-style international robotics competition in which one team per nation builds and programs a robot to tackle a global challenge theme.
Notes: Participation is via each country's national FIRST Global team. The 2026 event is in Incheon, Korea (theme: Igniting Innovation).
Subject: Engineering & Robotics

Name: The Hague International Model United Nations (THIMUN)
Eligibility: Secondary-school students in registered school delegations; students from 100+ countries.
Description: One of the world's largest and most international Model UN conferences: a four-day simulation of the UN for secondary-school students, held annually in The Hague.
Event_Dates: UNKNOWN (late January each year, The Hague)
Notes: Schools register delegations; about 3,200 participants from ~200 schools across 100+ countries. Held at the end of January in The Hague.
Subject: Social Sciences & Humanities

Name: Yale Model United Nations (YMUN)
Eligibility: High school students worldwide, individually or in school delegations; about 40 countries represented.
Description: Four-day international-relations simulation for high school students held on Yale's campus in New Haven, one of the largest high school Model UN conferences.
Notes: Held each January at Yale; 1,800+ delegates from 40+ countries. YIRA also runs YMUN conferences abroad (Korea, Dubai, Taiwan, Europe, Singapore).
Subject: Social Sciences & Humanities

Name: Rise (Rise for the World)
Eligibility: Young people aged 15-17 working in service of others; open to applicants worldwide.
Description: Global program that selects promising 15-17-year-olds and supports them for life with need-based scholarships, mentorship, funding, and career development.
Notes: Applications run on an annual cycle (historically opening in the fall/winter). 100 Global Winners are selected each year and receive lifelong benefits. Open worldwide.
Subject: Interdisciplinary

Name: TÜBİTAK National Mathematics Olympiad (Ulusal Matematik Olimpiyatı)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye) — BİDEB
Eligibility: Students enrolled at schools in Türkiye (roughly grades 9-11 for the high-school branches; final-year students are generally excluded from the international-team track).
Description: Türkiye's national mathematics olympiad, run by TÜBİTAK as a two-stage exam: a multiple-choice first stage held across all 81 provinces (and the TRNC), then a written/practical second stage. Top performers train at national team camps and are selected for Türkiye's team to the International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO).
Notes: Part of the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (TÜBİTAK 2202, run by BİDEB). The 2026 first-stage exam was held on 16 May 2026; application dates are announced each year through ebideb.tubitak.gov.tr.
Subject: Mathematics

Name: TÜBİTAK National Physics Olympiad (Ulusal Fizik Olimpiyatı)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye) — BİDEB
Eligibility: Students enrolled at schools in Türkiye (roughly grades 9-11 for the high-school branches; final-year students are generally excluded from the international-team track).
Description: Türkiye's national physics olympiad, run by TÜBİTAK as a two-stage exam: a multiple-choice first stage held across all 81 provinces (and the TRNC), then a written/practical second stage. Top performers train at national team camps and are selected for Türkiye's team to the International Physics Olympiad (IPhO).
Notes: Part of the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (TÜBİTAK 2202, run by BİDEB). The 2026 first-stage exam was held on 16 May 2026; application dates are announced each year through ebideb.tubitak.gov.tr.
Subject: Physics

Name: TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad (Ulusal Kimya Olimpiyatı)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye) — BİDEB
Eligibility: Students enrolled at schools in Türkiye (roughly grades 9-11 for the high-school branches; final-year students are generally excluded from the international-team track).
Description: Türkiye's national chemistry olympiad, run by TÜBİTAK as a two-stage exam: a multiple-choice first stage held across all 81 provinces (and the TRNC), then a written/practical second stage. Top performers train at national team camps and are selected for Türkiye's team to the International Chemistry Olympiad (IChO).
Notes: Part of the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (TÜBİTAK 2202, run by BİDEB). The 2026 first-stage exam was held on 16 May 2026; application dates are announced each year through ebideb.tubitak.gov.tr.
Subject: Chemistry

Name: TÜBİTAK National Informatics Olympiad (Ulusal Bilgisayar Olimpiyatı)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye) — BİDEB
Eligibility: Students enrolled at schools in Türkiye (roughly grades 9-11 for the high-school branches; final-year students are generally excluded from the international-team track).
Description: Türkiye's national informatics/computing olympiad, run by TÜBİTAK as a two-stage exam: a multiple-choice first stage held across all 81 provinces (and the TRNC), then a written/practical second stage. Top performers train at national team camps and are selected for Türkiye's team to the International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI).
Notes: Part of the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (TÜBİTAK 2202, run by BİDEB). The 2026 first-stage exam was held on 16 May 2026; application dates are announced each year through ebideb.tubitak.gov.tr.
Subject: Computer Science

Name: TÜBİTAK National Biology Olympiad (Ulusal Biyoloji Olimpiyatı)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye) — BİDEB
Eligibility: Students enrolled at schools in Türkiye (roughly grades 9-11 for the high-school branches; final-year students are generally excluded from the international-team track).
Description: Türkiye's national biology olympiad, run by TÜBİTAK as a two-stage exam: a multiple-choice first stage held across all 81 provinces (and the TRNC), then a written/practical second stage. Top performers train at national team camps and are selected for Türkiye's team to the International Biology Olympiad (IBO).
Notes: Part of the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (TÜBİTAK 2202, run by BİDEB). The 2026 first-stage exam was held on 16 May 2026; application dates are announced each year through ebideb.tubitak.gov.tr.
Subject: Biology

Name: Türkiye Astronomy and Astrophysics Olympiad (TROAA)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK — Türkiye Astronomy and Astrophysics Olympiads (TROAA)
Eligibility: Students at schools in Türkiye who are high-school students (or graduate in the competition year) and under 20 as of 1 July of the competition year.
Description: Türkiye's national astronomy and astrophysics olympiad. Candidates are ranked on theoretical, data-analysis and observational performance; the top five form the national team (with a reserve) and attend team camps before the international final.
Notes: Run within the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (astronomy-astrophysics branch); TROAA coordinates the selection and team camps for the International Olympiad on Astronomy and Astrophysics (IOAA). Application dates are announced annually.
Subject: Earth & Space; Physics

Name: TÜBİTAK National Geography Olympiad (Ulusal Coğrafya Olimpiyatı)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye) — BİDEB
Eligibility: Students enrolled at schools in Türkiye (roughly grades 9-11 for the high-school branches; final-year students are generally excluded from the international-team track).
Description: Türkiye's national geography olympiad, run by TÜBİTAK as a two-stage exam: a multiple-choice first stage held across all 81 provinces (and the TRNC), then a written/practical second stage. Top performers train at national team camps and are selected for Türkiye's team to the International Geography Olympiad (iGeo).
Notes: Part of the TÜBİTAK Science Olympiads (TÜBİTAK 2202, run by BİDEB). The 2026 first-stage exam was held on 16 May 2026; application dates are announced each year through ebideb.tubitak.gov.tr. Türkiye's iGeo team is selected through national geography selection, coordinated with the Turkish Geographical Society (Türk Coğrafya Kurumu, TCK), an IGU member.
Subject: Earth & Space; Social Sciences & Humanities

Name: TÜBİTAK 2204-A High School Students Research Projects Competition (Lise Öğrencileri Araştırma Projeleri Yarışması)
Organizer: TÜBİTAK (Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye)
Eligibility: High-school students at schools in Türkiye, entering an original research project (individually or in pairs, with an advisor).
Description: Türkiye's national high-school research-project competition, judged through regional and final stages across many subject areas. The strongest projects are selected to represent Türkiye at the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF).
Notes: Türkiye's ISEF-affiliated fair. Projects advance through preliminary/regional and final evaluations; top projects are chosen to compete at ISEF. Application timing is announced annually by TÜBİTAK.
Subject: Interdisciplinary
